prompt: Camera move left, pull out, then pan left. A narrow ledge of pale stone hangs above a canyon with no visible floor, its walls layered like folded parchment and threaded with roots that bloom into glass leaves. On the near side, a black pool reflects a sky that belongs to another hour, while farther away, bridges loop between freestanding cliff fragments in impossible but readable tiers. Thin deer with lantern antlers stand among white ferns, all facing a vertical slit of gold light suspended in the void. Dust, pollen, and drifting petals reveal depth between the ledge, the broken spans, and the receding chasm.

Camera move right, pull out, then pan right. A dreamlike valley opens as a sequence of precise terraces carved into lavender stone, each terrace holding a different season yet aligned by clear paths, stairways, and water channels. A house with four facades shares one foundation at the center, its doors leading onto mutually impossible courtyards that still connect through arches and bridges. Above it, a river rises from a cistern and pours upward into a ring-shaped cloud. Reed beds sway along a silver shoreline, moths the size of hands circle amber lantern trees, and a distant mountain turns inside out to expose a glowing interior ridge.

Camera push in. Suspended over a blue-white abyss, a floating city assembles itself from massive islands chained by elegant viaducts, each landmass carrying a distinct district. Closest is an observatory terrace of dark brass and marble, where armillary towers tilt toward a sun split into concentric halos. Beyond it, gardens spill from hanging courtyards, staircases descend into open air before meeting lower plazas, and a citadel rises from the largest island with buttresses rooted in cloud. Small ferries drift between docks carved into the rock undersides, while banners and birds trace the vertical scale toward the radiant heart of the city.

Camera pull out. At the center of a silent forest, a circular clearing breaks open into a perfectly smooth void, its edge bordered by cypress trunks that bend inward without falling. The ground around it is patterned with moss, salt, and fossil-like spirals, as though tides once crossed the trees. Farther out, the forest gives way to stepped canyons lit from below by a soft crimson glow, each terrace lined with standing stones and pools that mirror stars in daylight. A single beam of white light descends onto a weathered chair beside the rim, making the nearby leaves shine silver while everything beyond fades into layered dusk.
